segments.text: The Lord told Moses,
Tell the Israelites: When you make a special promise to dedicate someone to the Lord, these are the values you are to use.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: The value of a man aged twenty to sixty is fifty shekels of silver (using the sanctuary shekel standard).
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The value of a woman is thirty shekels.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The value of someone aged five to twenty is twenty shekels for a male and ten shekels for a female.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The value of someone aged one month to five years is five shekels of silver for a male and three shekels of silver for a female.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The value of someone aged sixty or older is fifteen shekels for a male and ten shekels of silver for a female.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: However, if when you fulfill your promise you are poorer than the fixed value, you are to present the person before the priest, who will then set the value depending on what you can afford.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If when you fulfill your promise you bring an animal that is permitted as an offering to the Lord, the animal given to the Lord shall be considered holy.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You are not allowed to replace it or swap it, either for one that is better or one that is worse. However, if you do replace it then both animals become holy.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If when you fulfill your promise you bring any unclean animal that is not permitted as an offering to the Lord, then you must show the animal to the priest.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The priest will decide its value, whether high or low. Whatever value the priest places on it is final.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you then decide to buy the animal back, you have to add one-fifth to its value in payment.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you dedicate your house as holy to the Lord, then the priest will decide its value, whether high or low. Whatever value the priest places on it remains final.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if you want to buy back your house, you have to add one-fifth to its value in payment. Then it will belong to you again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you dedicate some of your land to the Lord, then its value shall be determined by the amount of seed required to sow it: fifty shekels of silver for every homer of barley seed used.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you dedicate your field during the Jubilee Year, the value will be the full amount calculated.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if you dedicate your field after the Jubilee, the priest will work out the value depending on the number of years left until the next Jubilee Year, so reducing the value.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if you want to buy your field back, you have to add one-fifth to its value in payment. Then it will belong to you again.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if you don't buy the field back, or if you've already sold it to someone else, it can't ever be bought back.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When the Jubilee comes, the field will become holy, in the same way as a field devoted to the Lord. It will become the property of the priests.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you dedicate to the Lord a field you've bought that was not part of your original property,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: the priest will work out the value until the next Jubilee Year. You will pay on that day the exact value, giving it as a holy offering to the Lord.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: In the Jubilee Year, ownership of the field shall revert back to the person you bought it from—to the original owner of the land.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: (All values will use the sanctuary shekel standard of twenty gerahs to the shekel.)
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No one is allowed to dedicate the firstborn of the livestock, because the firstborn belongs to the Lord. Whether they are cattle, sheep, or goats, they are the Lord's.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But if it is an unclean animal, then you can buy it back according to its value, adding on one-fifth extra. If it's not bought back, then it is to be sold according to its value.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Anything that you specially dedicate[^53] to the Lord from what you own, whether it's a person, an animal, or your land, can't be sold or bought back. Anything specially dedicated is most holy to the Lord.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No one who is specially dedicated to destruction can be bought back. They must be killed.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Tithe from your crops or fruit belongs to the Lord; it is holy to the Lord.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: If you want to buy back some of your tithe, you must add on one-fifth to its value.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When you count your herds and flocks, every tenth animal that passes under the shepherd's rod is holy to the Lord.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: You must not examine it to see if it's good or bad, and you must not replace it. However, if you do replace it then both animals become holy; they can't be bought back.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: These are the laws the Lord gave to Moses for the Israelites on Mount Sinai.
